influence_score.4: major movement, policy, academic, or public-discourse influence
influence_score.3: important niche, memetic, or genealogical influence

thinkers.name: Eliezer Yudkowsky
thinkers.stance_summary: Advanced AI is likely to kill humanity unless alignment is solved before deployment.

spectrum_position.notes: Hard doomer; human-protective but not primarily democratic or justice-theoretic.

representative_works.title: Artificial Intelligence as a Positive and Negative Factor in Global Risk
representative_works.why: Canonical early rationalist statement of AI as existential risk.

representative_works.title: AGI Ruin: A List of Lethalities
representative_works.why: Most compact hard-doomer public argument.

representative_works.title: Pausing AI Developments Isn't Enough. We Need to Shut It All Down
representative_works.why: Mainstream public-policy version of the shutdown argument.

thinkers.name: Nick Bostrom
thinkers.stance_summary: Superintelligent agents may pursue convergent instrumental goals that threaten human control.

spectrum_position.notes: Human-survival oriented philosophical x-risk frame.

representative_works.title: Ethical Issues in Advanced Artificial Intelligence
representative_works.why: Early philosophical framing of advanced AI ethics and risk.

representative_works.title: The Superintelligent Will: Motivation and Instrumental Rationality in Advanced Artificial Agents
representative_works.why: Primary source for instrumental convergence.

representative_works.title: Superintelligence: Paths, Dangers, Strategies
representative_works.why: Book-length field-defining x-risk argument.

thinkers.name: Stuart Russell
thinkers.stance_summary: AI systems should be designed around uncertainty about human preferences and provable benefit to humans.

spectrum_position.notes: Strongly safety-oriented and explicitly human-compatible.

representative_works.title: Human Compatible: Artificial Intelligence and the Problem of Control
representative_works.why: Main public statement of the control-problem and beneficial-AI frame.

representative_works.title: Provably Beneficial Artificial Intelligence
representative_works.why: Useful entry into the technical version of human-compatible AI.

thinkers.name: Paul Christiano
thinkers.stance_summary: Advanced AI failure may arise through gradual delegation, misalignment, and scalable oversight failures.

spectrum_position.notes: Technical alignment position; less rhetorically apocalyptic than hard-doomer writing.

representative_works.title: What Failure Looks Like
representative_works.why: Canonical gradualist failure-mode text.

representative_works.title: AI Safety via Debate
representative_works.why: Representative scalable oversight proposal.

thinkers.name: Ajeya Cotra
thinkers.stance_summary: Transformative AI timelines can be forecast using compute requirements and biological anchors.

spectrum_position.notes: Not a full social theory of AI; important because timelines structure safety urgency.

representative_works.title: Forecasting Transformative AI with Biological Anchors
representative_works.why: Influential compute-based AI timelines report.

representative_works.title: Forecasting Transformative AI: The Biological Anchors Method in a Nutshell
representative_works.why: Accessible summary of the timeline method.

thinkers.name: Dario Amodei / Anthropic
thinkers.stance_summary: AI could bring enormous benefits, but frontier scaling requires explicit safety, evals, and deployment thresholds.

spectrum_position.notes: Combines safety urgency with continued frontier-model development.

representative_works.title: Core Views on AI Safety
representative_works.why: Company-level view of risk, alignment, and responsible scaling.

representative_works.title: Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy
representative_works.why: Important institutionalization of eval-triggered safety governance.

representative_works.title: Machines of Loving Grace
representative_works.why: Most explicit high-upside, safety-aware frontier-lab optimism essay.

thinkers.name: Dan Hendrycks / Center for AI Safety
thinkers.cluster: Public AI x-risk and ML safety
thinkers.stance_summary: AI should be treated as a societal-scale risk comparable to other major global hazards.

spectrum_position.notes: Bridges benchmark-oriented ML safety and public extinction-risk messaging.

representative_works.title: Unsolved Problems in ML Safety
representative_works.why: Technical safety agenda source.

representative_works.title: Statement on AI Risk
representative_works.why: Canonical compact public extinction-risk statement.

thinkers.name: Emily Bender, Timnit Gebru, Margaret Mitchell, and Angelina McMillan-Major
thinkers.stance_summary: Large language models carry social, environmental, labor, data, and representational harms that are obscured by scale-centered narratives.

spectrum_position.notes: Present-harm critical AI position, distinct from AGI x-risk safety.

representative_works.title: On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?
representative_works.why: Canonical critical-AI critique of large language models.

thinkers.name: Kate Crawford
thinkers.stance_summary: AI is a material, labor, data, and power infrastructure rather than a disembodied intelligence.

spectrum_position.notes: Critical political economy of AI; focused on institutions and extraction.

representative_works.title: Atlas of AI
representative_works.why: Book-length material and political-economy critique.

representative_works.title: Excavating AI
representative_works.why: Concrete genealogy of image datasets, classification, and power.

thinkers.name: Ruha Benjamin
thinkers.stance_summary: Automated systems can reproduce and intensify racial hierarchy while presenting themselves as neutral or objective.

spectrum_position.notes: Broader technology-and-race frame that should anchor the justice side of the map.

representative_works.title: Race After Technology
representative_works.why: Important justice-oriented technology critique relevant to AI governance.

thinkers.name: Arvind Narayanan and Sayash Kapoor
thinkers.stance_summary: AI is powerful but often oversold; evaluation should distinguish real capability from snake oil and from magical AGI narratives.

spectrum_position.notes: Hype-skeptical, evidence-first, and often skeptical of x-risk exceptionalism.

representative_works.title: AI Snake Oil
representative_works.why: Accessible normal-technology and anti-hype frame.

representative_works.title: AI as Normal Technology
representative_works.why: Concise counterweight to both utopian and catastrophic exceptionalism.

thinkers.name: Gary Marcus / Melanie Mitchell
thinkers.stance_summary: Current deep learning is impressive but brittle, and may not be sufficient for robust general intelligence.

spectrum_position.notes: Capability-skeptical rather than primarily political.

representative_works.title: Deep Learning: A Critical Appraisal
representative_works.why: Canonical skeptical critique of deep learning generality.

representative_works.title: Why AI Is Harder Than We Think
representative_works.why: Readable critique of repeated overclaims in AI.

thinkers.name: Rich Sutton
thinkers.stance_summary: General methods that leverage search, learning, and computation ultimately beat attempts to encode human domain knowledge.

spectrum_position.notes: Not an AI policy position; anti-anthropocentric in technical epistemology.

representative_works.title: The Bitter Lesson
representative_works.why: Canonical statement of the scaling/general-methods lesson.

thinkers.name: Yann LeCun
thinkers.stance_summary: Advanced AI needs new architectures and world models; near-term doomer narratives are overstated.

spectrum_position.notes: Build-oriented technical optimism, but not simply e/acc.

representative_works.title: A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence
representative_works.why: Representative architecture-centered view of future AI.

thinkers.name: Andrew Ng
thinkers.stance_summary: AI is a general-purpose technology like electricity, and should be deployed broadly to create value.

spectrum_position.notes: Deployment-focused optimism and economic/productivity framing.

representative_works.title: AI is the New Electricity
representative_works.why: Canonical pragmatic business and productivity frame.

thinkers.name: Sam Altman / OpenAI
thinkers.stance_summary: AGI can produce broad abundance if developed with safety, governance, and benefit-sharing institutions.

spectrum_position.notes: Frontier-lab abundance frame with explicit institutional safety language.

representative_works.title: OpenAI Charter
representative_works.why: Core institutional statement about AGI and benefit-sharing.

representative_works.title: Planning for AGI and Beyond
representative_works.why: Public OpenAI view on AGI development, safety, and governance.

representative_works.title: The Intelligence Age
representative_works.why: Concise abundance-oriented AGI optimism.

thinkers.name: Ray Kurzweil
thinkers.stance_summary: Technological progress, especially AI and computing, points toward a singularity and human-machine merger.

spectrum_position.notes: Optimistic and partly posthuman, but not Land-style anti-humanism.

representative_works.title: The Singularity Is Near
representative_works.why: Canonical modern singularitarian optimism.

representative_works.title: The Singularity Is Nearer
representative_works.why: Updated public singularity thesis.

thinkers.name: Marc Andreessen
thinkers.stance_summary: AI should be developed aggressively because it can expand abundance, solve problems, and empower people.

spectrum_position.notes: Techno-optimist and anti-regulatory, but framed as pro-human abundance.

representative_works.title: Why AI Will Save the World
representative_works.why: Canonical contemporary pro-AI anti-doomer essay.

representative_works.title: The Techno-Optimist Manifesto
representative_works.why: Broader ideology behind the AI optimism argument.

thinkers.name: e/acc / effective accelerationism
thinkers.stance_summary: Technological acceleration is treated as morally urgent and regulation is often treated as the larger danger.

spectrum_position.notes: Memetic and political more than academic; varies by author.

representative_works.title: Effective Accelerationism
representative_works.why: Movement source for contemporary e/acc framing.

thinkers.name: Nick Land
thinkers.stance_summary: Capital, technology, and intelligence are imagined as autonomous runaway processes that exceed humanist control.

spectrum_position.notes: Important as accelerationist genealogy and anti-humanist edge; not mainstream AI technical policy.

representative_works.title: Meltdown
representative_works.why: Canonical cybernetic/accelerationist anti-humanist text.

representative_works.title: The Dark Enlightenment
representative_works.why: Key reactionary-political branch of Land's later thought; include with caution.

movement_documents.title: Pause Giant AI Experiments: An Open Letter
movement_documents.why: Movement document for the pause position.

movement_documents.title: The Bletchley Declaration
movement_documents.why: Government-level institutionalization of frontier-AI safety discourse.
